cable_car.description: A cable car run. Just one or two large cars. The traction cable forms a loop, but the cars do not loop around, they just move up and down on their own side, rolling along static cables over which they are suspended.

chair_lift.description: An open chairlift run. These have one or more seats or benches and are open to the outside air.

drag_lift.description: An overhead tow-line for skiers and riders.

gondola.description: An aerialway where the cabins go around in a circle.

goods.description: A cable supported lift for conveying goods, not passengers.

j-bar.description: J-bar lift or L-bar lift. Overhead tow-line for skiers and riders with carriers in J-shape.

magic_carpet.description: A ski lift for small children resembling a conveyor belt.

mixed_lift.description: A mixed lift, containing both gondolas and chairs.

platter.description: An overhead tow-line for skiers and riders with platters.

pylon.description: A pylon supporting the aerialway cable.

rope_tow.description: A tow-line for skiers and riders where passenger hold by hand or use special tow grabbers.

station.description: A station where passengers and/or goods can enter and/or leave the aerialway

t-bar.description: An overhead tow-line for skiers and riders with T-shaped carriers for two passengers.

zip_line.description: Zip line, gravity propelled aerial ropeslide like a flying fox or similar

aircraft_crossing.description: A point where the flow of traffic is impacted by crossing aircraft.

apron.description: An area of an airport where planes are parked, unloaded, loaded, refueled, or boarded.

gate.description: The bounded space, inside the airport terminal, where passengers wait before boarding their flight

hangar.description: A large airport building with extensive floor area that is used for housing aircraft or spacecraft.

helipad.description: A landing area or platform designed for helicopters. It might be part of an aerodrome, heliport, hospital, hotel etc.

heliport.description: A special aerodrome built for helicopters. A heliport will usually have one or more helipads.

navigationaid.description: A facility that supports visual navigation for aircraft

runway.description: A designated strip of surface on which aircrafts can take off and land.

spaceport.description: A spaceport or cosmodrome: a site for launching or receiving spacecraft

taxiway.description: A route where airplanes manouevre between runways and parking areas within an aerodrome

windsock.description: An object that shows wind direction and speed.

animal_boarding.description: A facility where you, paying a fee, can bring your animal for a limited period of time (e.g. for holidays)

arts_centre.description: A venue where a variety of arts are performed or conducted

atm.description: Automated teller machine (ATM) or cashpoint: a device that provides the clients of a financial institution with access to financial transactions.

baby_hatch.description: A place where a baby can be, out of necessity, anonymously left to be safely cared for and perhaps adopted.

bar.description: Baris a purpose-built commercial establishment that sells alcoholic drinks to be consumed on the premises. They are characterised by a noisy and vibrant atmosphere, similar to a party and usually don't sell food. See also the description of the tagsamenity=pub;bar;restaurantfor a distinction between

bbq.description: BBQorBarbecueis a permanently built grill for cooking food, which is most typically used outdoors by the public. For example these may be found in city parks or at beaches. Use the tagfuel=*to specify the source of heating, such asfuel=wood;electric;charcoal. For mapping nearby table and chairs, see

college.description: Campus or buildings of an institute of Further Education (aka continuing education)

community_centre.description: A place mostly used for local events, festivities and group activities; including special interest and special age groups. .

courthouse.description: A building home to a court of law, where justice is dispensed

sanitary_dump_station.description: A place for depositing human waste from a toilet holding tank.

school.description: School and grounds - primary, middle and secondary schools

university.description: A university campus: an institute of higher education

animal_breeding.description: A facility where animals are bred, usually to sell them

animal_shelter.description: A shelter that recovers animals in trouble

animal_training.description: A facility used for non-competitive animal training

baking_oven.description: An oven used for baking bread and similar, for example inside abuilding=bakehouse.

bank.description: Bank or credit union: a financial establishment where customers can deposit and withdraw money, take loans, make investments and transfer funds.

bench.description: A bench to sit down and relax a bit

bicycle_repair_station.description: General tools for self-service bicycle repairs, usually on the roadside; no service...

biergarten.description: Biergartenorbeer gardenis an open-air area where alcoholic beverages along with food is prepared and served. See also the description of the tagsamenity=pub;bar;restaurant. A biergarten can commonly be found attached to a beer hall, pub, bar, or restaurant. In this case, you can usebiergarten=yesadditional toamenity=pub;bar;restaurant.

boat_storage.description: A place to store boats out of the water.

brothel.description: An establishment specifically dedicated to prostitution

bureau_de_change.description: Bureau de change, money changer, currency exchange, Wechsel, cambio – a place to change foreign bank notes and travellers cheques.

cafe.description: Cafeis generally an informal place that offers casual meals and beverages; typically, the focus is on coffee or tea. Also known as acoffeehouse/shop,bistroorsidewalk cafe. The kind of food served may be mapped with the tagscuisine=*anddiet:*=*. See also the tagsamenity=restaurant;bar;fast_food.

casino.description: A gambling venue with at least one table game(e.g. roulette, blackjack) that takes bets on sporting and other events at agreed upon odds.

check_in.description: Place where passengers can get their boarding passes before travel (typically found in airports).

cinema.description: A place where films are shown (US: movie theater)

clinic.description: A medium-sized medical facility or health centre.

compressed_air.description: A device to inflate tires/tyres (e.g. motorcar, bicycle)

conference_centre.description: A large building that is used to hold a convention

crematorium.description: A place where dead human bodies are burnt

dancing_school.description: A dancing school or dance studio

dive_centre.description: A dive center is the base location where sports divers usually start scuba diving or make dive guided trips at new locations.

dog_toilet.description: Area designated for dogs to urinate and excrete.

drinking_water.description: Drinking wateris a place where humans can obtain potable water for consumption. Typically, the water is used for only drinking. Also known as adrinking fountainorbubbler.

driver_training.description: A place for driving training on a closed course

driving_school.description: Driving School which offers motor vehicle driving lessons

events_venue.description: A building specifically used for organising events

fast_food.description: Fast food restaurant (see alsoamenity=restaurant). The kind of food served can be tagged withcuisine=*anddiet:*=*.

ferry_terminal.description: Ferry terminal/stop. A place where people/cars/etc. can board and leave a ferry.

fire_station.description: A station of a fire brigade

first_aid_school.description: A place where people can go for first aid courses.

food_court.description: An area with several different restaurant food counters and a shared eating area. Commonly found in malls, airports, etc.

fountain.description: A fountain for cultural / decorational / recreational purposes.

fuel.description: Petrol station; gas station; marine fuel; … Streets to petrol stations are often taggedhighway=service.

funeral_hall.description: A place for holding a funeral ceremony, other than a place of worship.

gambling.description: A place for gambling, not being ashop=bookmaker,shop=lottery,amenity=casino, orleisure=adult_gaming_centre.Games that are covered by this definition include bingo and pachinko.

give_box.description: A small facility where people drop off and pick up various types of items in the sense of free sharing and reuse.

grave_yard.description: A (smaller) place of burial, often you'll find a church nearby. Large places should belanduse=cemeteryinstead.

grit_bin.description: A container that holds grit or a mixture of salt and grit.

hospital.description: A hospital providing in-patient medical treatment. Often used in conjunction withemergency=*to note whether the medical centre has emergency facilities (A&E (brit.) or ER (am.))

hunting_stand.description: A hunting stand: an open or enclosed platform used by hunters to place themselves at an elevated height above the terrain

hydrant.description: Similar to afire_hydrant=*, but for gardening and other municipal purposes other than fire extinction

ice_cream.description: Ice cream shop or ice cream parlour. A place that sells ice cream and frozen yoghurt over the counter

internet_cafe.description: A place whose principal role is providing internet services to the public.

kindergarten.description: For children too young for a regular school (also known as preschool, playschool or nursery school), in some countries including afternoon supervision of primary school children.

kitchen.description: A public kitchen in a facility to use by everyone or customers

kneipp_water_cure.description: Outdoor foot bath facility. Usually this is a pool with cold water and handrail. Popular in German speaking countries.

language_school.description: Language School: an educational institution where one studies a foreign language.

library.description: A public library (municipal, university, …) to borrow books from.

lounge.description: A comfortable waiting area for customers, usually found in airports and other transportation hubs. Typically has extra amenities or sustenance.

lounger.description: An object for people to lie down.

love_hotel.description: A love hotel is a type of short-stay hotel operated primarily for the purpose of allowing guests privacy for sexual activities.

mailroom.description: A mailroom for receiving packages or letters.

marketplace.description: A marketplace where goods and services are traded daily or weekly.

monastery.description: Monastery is the location of a monastery or a building in which monks and nuns live.

money_transfer.description: A place that offers money transfers, especially cash to cash

mortuary.description: A morgue or funeral home, used for the storage of human corpses.

music_school.description: A music school, an educational institution specialized in the study, training, and research of music.

music_venue.description: An indoor place to hear contemporary live music.

nightclub.description: A place to drink and dance (nightclub). The German word is "Disco" or "Discothek". Please don't confuse this with the German "Nachtclub" which is most likelyamenity=stripclub.

nursing_home.description: Discouraged tag for a home for disabled or elderly persons who need permanent care. Useamenity=social_facility+social_facility=nursing_homenow.

parcel_locker.description: Machine for picking up and sending parcels

parking.description: Parking area for vehicles. Streets on car parking are often taggedhighway=serviceandservice=parking_aisle.

parking_entrance.description: An entrance or exit to an underground or multi-storey parking facility. Group multiple parking entrances together with a relation using the tagstype=siteandsite=parking

parking_space.description: A single parking space within a car park. Parking spaces should be mapped within anamenity=parkingarea. Group multiple parking spaces together with a relation using the tagstype=siteandsite=parking

payment_centre.description: A non-bank place, where people can pay bills of public and private services and taxes.

pharmacy.description: Pharmacy: a shop where a pharmacist sells medicationsdispensing=yes/no- availability of prescription-only medications

photo_booth.description: A stand to create instant photos.

place_of_mourning.description: A room or building where families and friends can come, before the funeral, and view the body of the person who has died.

place_of_worship.description: A church, mosque, or temple, etc. Note that you also needreligion=*, usuallydenomination=*and preferablyname=*as well asamenity=place_of_worship. Seethe articlefor details.

police.description: A police station where police officers patrol from and that is a first point of contact for civilians

post_box.description: A box for the reception of mail. Alternative mail-carriers can be tagged viaoperator=*

post_depot.description: Post depot or delivery office, where letters and parcels are collected and sorted prior to delivery.

post_office.description: Post office building with postal services

prison.description: A prison or jail where people are incarcerated before trial or after conviction

pub.description: A place selling beer and other alcoholic drinks; may also provide food or accommodation (UK). See description ofamenity=barandamenity=pubfor distinction between bar and pub

public_bath.description: A location where the public may bathe in common, etc. japanese onsen, turkish bath, hot spring

public_bookcase.description: A street furniture containing books. Take one or leave one.

public_building.description: A generic public building. Don't use! Seeoffice=government.

ranger_station.description: National Park visitor headquarters: official park visitor facility with police, visitor information, permit services, etc

recycling.description: Recycling facilities (bottle banks, etc.). Combine withrecycling_type=containerfor containers orrecycling_type=centrefor recycling centres.

refugee_site.description: A human settlement sheltering refugees or internally displaced persons

research_institute.description: An establishment endowed for doing research.

restaurant.description: Restaurant (not fast food, seeamenity=fast_food). The kind of food served can be tagged withcuisine=*anddiet:*=*.

shelter.description: A small shelter against bad weather conditions. To additionally describe the kind of shelter useshelter_type=*.

social_centre.description: A place for free and not-for-profit activities.

social_facility.description: A facility that provides social services: group & nursing homes, workshops for the disabled, homeless shelters, etc.

stripclub.description: A place that offers striptease or lapdancing (for sexual services useamenity=brothel).

surf_school.description: A surf school is an establishment that teaches surfing.

swingerclub.description: A club where people meet to have a party and group sex.

taxi.description: A place where taxis wait for passengers.

theatre.description: A theatre or opera house where live performances occur, such as plays, musicals and formal concerts. Useamenity=cinemafor movie theaters.

townhall.description: Building where the administration of a village, town or city may be located, or just a community meeting place

toy_library.description: A place to borrow games and toys, or play with them on site.

training.description: Public place where you can get training.

vending_machine.description: A machine selling goods – food, tickets, newspapers, etc. Add type of goods usingvending=*

veterinary.description: A place where a veterinary surgeon, also known as a veterinarian or vet, practices.

waste_basket.description: A single small container for depositing garbage that is easily accessible for pedestrians.

waste_disposal.description: A medium or large disposal bin, typically for bagged up household or industrial waste.

waste_transfer_station.description: A waste transfer station is a location that accepts, consolidates and transfers waste in bulk.

water_point.description: Place where you can get large amounts of drinking water

watering_place.description: Place where water is contained and animals can drink

weighbridge.description: A large weight scale to weigh vehicles and goods

[en].description: Audiologist; if linked with a shop please useshop=hearing_aidsinstead

block.description: One or more large immobile block(s) usually barring free access along a way.

bollard.description: One or more solid (usually concrete or metal) pillar(s) used to control traffic.

border_control.description: A control point at an international border between two countries.

bump_gate.description: A drive-through gate used in rural areas to provide a barrier to livestock that does not require the driver to exit the vehicle.

bus_trap.description: A short section of the roadway where there is a deep dip in the middle to prevent passage by some traffic.

cable_barrier.description: A road side or median barrier made of steel wire ropes mounted on weak posts.

cattle_grid.description: A hole in the road surface covered in a series of bars that allow wheeled vehicles but not animals to cross.

chain.description: A chain used to prevent motorised vehicles.

city_wall.description: A fortification used to defend settlements in ancient to modern times.

cycle_barrier.description: A barrier along a path that slows or prevents access for bicycle users.

ditch.description: A ditch or a trench is a long and narrow man-made barrier dug in the ground to prevent access to the other side.

entrance.description: An opening or gap in a barrier.

fence.description: A freestanding structure designed to restrict or prevent movement across a boundary, generally distinguished from a wall by the lightness of its construction.

gate.description: A section in a wall or fence which can be opened to allow access.

guard_rail.description: A rail, typically metal, designed to keep vehicles within their roadway and prevent collisions or runaways. Right side faces outward. In iD, the "poles" on the left mark the protected side, and the smooth right side goes toward traffic.

hampshire_gate.description: A section of wire fence which can be removed temporarily.

handrail.description: A rail designed to be grasped by the hand so as to provide stability or support.

hedge.description: A line of closely spaced shrubs and tree species, which form a barrier or mark the boundary of an area.

height_restrictor.description: A height restrictor which prevents access of vehicles higher than a set limit.

horse_stile.description: A horse stile allows pedestrians and horses to cross a gap through a fence

jersey_barrier.description: A barrier made of heavy prefabricated blocks.

kerb.description: A stone edging to a pavement or raised path.

kissing_gate.description: A type of gate where you have to go into an enclosure and open a gate to get through.

lift_gate.description: A bar or pole pivoted (rotates upwards to open) in such a way as to allow the boom to block vehicular access through a controlled point.

motorcycle_barrier.description: A barrier along a path preventing access by motorcycles.

retaining_wall.description: A linear structure that holds back soil or rock. (Lower side to right.)

rope.description: A flexible barrier made of rope.

sally_port.description: A secure, controlled entryway to a fortification or prison.

spikes.description: Spikes on the ground preventing unauthorized access.

stile.description: A structure which provides people a passage through or over a boundary via steps, ladders or narrow gaps.

sump_buster.description: A barrier to stop cars (two tracked vehicles with less than a certain ground clearance and width between tracks).

swing_gate.description: A gate consisting of a bar or pole pivoted (rotates sidewards to open) in such a way as to allow the boom to block vehicular access through a controlled point.

toll_booth.description: A place where a road usage toll or fee is collected.

turnstile.description: A turnstile, a passage on foot designed to allow one person at a time to pass

wall.description: A freestanding structure, designed to restrict or prevent movement across a boundary, typically made from solid brick, concrete or rocks, and almost always built so that it is opaque to vision.

yes.description: Unspecified barrier. If possible, use a more specific value.

[en].description: A non-clinicalbirthing centreoperated by midwives.
A facility where you can donate blood, plasma and/or platelets, and possibly have stem cell samples taken.

aboriginal_lands.description: A boundary representing official reservation boundaries of recognized aboriginal / indigenous / native peoples.

administrative.description: An administrative boundary. Subdivisions of areas/territories/jurisdictions recognised by governments or other organisations for administrative purposes. These range from large groups of nation states right down to small administrative districts and suburbs, as indicated by the

census.description: A census-designated boundary delineating a statistical area, not necessarily observable on the ground.

disputed.description: An area of landed claimed by two or more parties (use with caution). See also

forest.description: A delimited forest is a land which is predominantly wooded and which is, for this reason, given defined boundaries. It may cover different tree stands, non-wooded areas, highways… but all the area within the boundaries are considered and managed as a single forest.

forest_compartment.description: A forest compartment is a numbered sub-division within a delimited forest, physically materialized with visible, typically cleared, boundaries.

health.description: Health division boundaries. Some mappers use

limited_traffic_zone.description: Describes an officially designated area that requires authorization for the entry of certain vehicles.

local_authority.description: Describes the territory of a local authority.

low_emission_zone.description: A geographically defined area which seeks to restrict or deter access by certain polluting vehicles with the aim of improving the air quality.

national_park.description: Area of outstanding natural beauty, set aside for conservation and for recreation (

postal_code.description: Postal code boundaries. Some mappers use

public_transport.description: An operating area of a transit authority.

special_economic_zone.description: A government-defined area in which business and trade laws are different.

statistical.description: An official boundary recognised by government for statistical purposes.

border_zone.description: A border zone is an area near the border where special restrictions on movement apply. Usually a permit is required for visiting.

hazard.description: A designated hazardous area, with a potential source of damage to health, life, property, or any other interest of value.

maritime.description: Maritime boundaries which are not administrative boundaries: the Baseline, Contiguous Zone and EEZ (Exclusive Economic Zone).

marker.description: A boundary marker, border marker, boundary stone, or border stone is a robust physical marker that identifies the start of a land boundary or the change in a boundary, especially a change in direction of a boundary. See alsohistoric=boundary_stone

protected_area.description: Protected areas, such as for national parks, marine protection areas, heritage sites, wilderness, cultural assets and similar.

religious_administration.description: A religious administration boundary, eg. of a catholic diocese or parish. Usereligion=*anddenomination=*to specify the church to which the boundary applies. Some mappers usereligious_level=*instead ofadmin_level=*to specify level in the church hierarchy.

allotment_house.description: A small outbuilding for short visits in a allotment garden.

apartments.description: A building arranged into individual dwellings, often on separate floors. May also have retail outlets on the ground floor.

bakehouse.description: A building that was built as a bakehouse (i.e. for baking bread). Often used in conjunction with another nodeamenity=baking_ovenandoven=wood_fired.

barn.description: An agricultural building that can be used for storage and as a covered workplace.

beach_hut.description: A small, usually wooden, and often brightly coloured cabin or shelter above the high tide mark on popular bathing beaches.

carport.description: Acarportis a covered structure used to offer limited protection to vehicles, primarily cars, from the elements. Unlike most structures a carport does not have four walls, and usually has one or two.

commercial.description: A building for non-specific commercial activities, not necessarily an office building. Consider tagging the surrounding area usinglanduse=commercialif there is such use. Use 'retail' if the building consists primarily of shops.

digester.description: A digester is a bioreactor for the production of biogas from biomass.

grandstand.description: The main stand, usually roofed, commanding the best view for spectators at racecourses or sports grounds.

religious.description: Unspecific building related to religion. Prefer more specific values if possible.

[[Too many Data Items entities accessed..description: Use this value where it is not possible to determine a more specific value.

annexe.description: An annexe is a small self contained apartments, collages, or small residential building on the same property as the main residential unit.

barracks.description: Buildings built to house military personnel or laborers.

boathouse.description: A boathouse is a building used for the storage of boats.

bridge.description: A building used as a bridge (skyway). To map a gatehouse usebuilding=gatehouse. See alsobridge=yesforhighway=*.Don't use this tag just for marking bridges (their outlines). For such purposes useman_made=bridge.

bungalow.description: A single-storey detached small house, Dacha.

bunker.description: A hardened military building. Also usemilitary=bunker.

cabin.description: Acabinis a small, roughly built house usually with a wood exterior and typically found in rural areas.

castle.description: A building constructed as a castle. Can be further specified withhistoric=castle+castle_type=*.

cathedral.description: A building that was built as a cathedral. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the cathedral grounds where it is in current use.

chapel.description: A building that was built as a chapel. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the chapel grounds where it is in current use.

church.description: A building that was built as a church. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the church grounds where it is in current use.

civic.description: A generic tag for a building created to house some civic amenity, for exampleamenity=community_centre,amenity=library,amenity=toilets,leisure=sports_centre,leisure=swimming_pool,amenity=townhalletc. Useamenity=*orleisure=*etc. to provide further details. Seebuilding=publicand more specific tags likebuilding=libraryas well.

clock_tower.description: Clock towers are very high towers, visible from its surroundings, usually in central parts of town and squares, housing a turret clock with one or more clock faces, most often four. They are freestanding structures but can also be adjoined or located on top of another building.

college.description: A college building. Useamenity=collegeon the whole college area to represent the college as an institution.

conservatory.description: A building or room having glass or tarpaulin roofing and walls used as an indoor garden or a sunroom (winter garden).

construction.description: Used for buildings under construction. Useconstruction=*to hold the value for the completed building.

container.description: For a container used as a permanent building.Do not map containers placed temporarily, for example used in shipping or construction.

cowshed.description: A cowshed (cow barn, cow house) is a building for housing cows, usually found on farms.

detached.description: A detached house, a free-standing residential building usually housing a single family.

dormitory.description: A shared building intended for college/university students (not a share room for multiple occupants as implied by the term in British English). Alternatively, use building=residential plusresidential=universityand loose the information that it is for students.

farm.description: A residential building on a farm (farmhouse). For other buildings see belowbuilding=farm_auxiliary,building=barn, … If in your country farmhouse looks same as general residential house then you can tag asbuilding=houseas well. See alsolanduse=farmyardfor the surrounding area

farm_auxiliary.description: A building on a farm that is not a dwelling (use 'farm' or 'house' for the farm house).

fire_station.description: A building constructed as fire station, i.e. to house fire fighting equipment and officers, regardless of current use. Addamenity=fire_stationon the grounds for an active fire station.

garage.description: Agarageis a building suitable for the storage of one or possibly more motor vehicle or similar. Seebuilding=garagesfor larger shared buildings. For an aircraft garage, seebuilding=hangar.

garages.description: A building that consists of a number of discrete storage spaces for different owners/tenants. See alsobuilding=garage.

gatehouse.description: An entry control point building, spanning over a highway that enters a city or compound.

ger.description: A permanent or seasonal round yurt or ger.

government.description: For government buildings in general, including municipal, provincial and divisional secretaries, government agencies and departments, town halls, (regional) parliaments and court houses.

greenhouse.description: Agreenhouseis a glass or plastic covered building used to grow plants. Uselanduse=greenhouse_horticulturefor an area containing multiple greenhouses

hangar.description: Ahangaris a building used for the storage of airplanes, helicopters or space-craft. Consider addingaeroway=hangar, when appropriate.

hospital.description: A building errected for a hospital. Useamenity=hospitalfor the hospital grounds.

hotel.description: A building designed with separate rooms available for overnight accommodation. Normally used in conjunction withtourism=hotelfor the hotel grounds including recreation areas and parking.

house.description: A dwelling unit inhabited by a single household (a family or small group sharing facilities such as a kitchen). Houses forming half of a semi-detached pair, or one of a row of terraced houses, should share at least two nodes with joined neighbours, thereby defining theparty wallbetween the properties.

houseboat.description: A boat used primarily as a home

hut.description: Ahutis a small and crude shelter. Note that this word hastwo meanings- it may be synonym ofbuilding=shed, it may be a residential building of low quality.

industrial.description: A building for industrial purposes. Use warehouse if the purpose is known to be primarily for storage/distribution. Consider usinglanduse=industrialfor the surrounding area and appropriate tags likeman_made=worksto describe the industrial activity.

kindergarten.description: For any generic kindergarten buildings. Buildings for specific uses (sports halls etc.) should be tagged for their purpose. If there is currently a kindergarten in the building, useamenity=kindergartenon the perimeter of the kindergarten grounds.

kingdom_hall.description: A building that was built as aKingdom Hall. Often used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=christian,denomination=jehovahs_witnessandlanduse=religiousfor the grounds where it is in current use.

livestock.description: A building for housing/rising other livestock (apart from cows, horses or pigs covered above), or when the livestock changes.

monastery.description: A building constructed asmonastery. Often, monasteries consist of several distinct buildings with specific functions. For active monasteries (communities), useamenity=monastery, for the grounds of a former monastery, have a look athistoric=monastery

mosque.description: A building errected as mosque. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the grounds where it is in current use.

museum.description: A building which was designed as a museum.

office.description: An office building. Useoffice=*where applicable for the business(es) that use the building. Consider tagging the surrounding area usinglanduse=commercialif it applies. Preferlanduse=retailif the building consists primarily of shops.

outbuilding.description: A less important building near to and on the same piece of land as a larger building.

pagoda.description: A building constructed as a pagoda.

pavilion.description: A sports pavilion usually with changing rooms, storage areas and possibly an space for functions & events. Avoid using this term for other structures called pavilions by architects (seePavilion)

presbytery.description: A building where priests live and work.

public.description: A building constructed as accessible to the general public (a town hall, police station, court house, etc.).

quonset_hut.description: A lightweight prefabricated structure in the shape of a semicircle.

residential.description: A general tag for a building used primarily for residential purposes. Where additional detail is available consider using 'apartments', 'terrace', 'house', 'detached' or 'semidetached_house'.

retail.description: A building primarily used for selling goods that are sold to the public; useshop=*to identify the sort of goods sold or an appropriateamenity=*(pub, cafe, restaurant, etc.). Consider uselanduse=retailfor the surrounding area.

riding_hall.description: A building that was built as a riding hall.

roof.description: A structure that consists of a roof with open sides, such as a rain shelter, and also gas stations

ruins.description: Frequently used for a house or other building that is abandoned and in poor repair. However, some believe this usage is incorrect, and the tag should only be used for buildings constructed as fake ruins (for example sham ruins in an English landscape garden). See alsolifecycle tagging.

school.description: A building errected as school. Buildings for specific uses (sports halls etc.) should be tagged for their purpose. If there is currently a school, useamenity=schoolon the  perimeter of the school grounds.

semidetached_house.description: A residential house that shares a common wall with another on one side. Typically called a "duplex" in American English.

service.description: Service building usually is a small unmanned building with certain machinery (like pumps or transformers).

shed.description: Ashedis a simple, single-storey structure that is used for storage, hobbies, or as a workshop.

ship.description: A decommissioned ship/submarine which stays in one place.

shrine.description: A building that was built as a shrine. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the grounds where it is in current use. Small structures should considerhistoric=wayside_shrine.

silo.description: A silo is a building for storing bulk materials

slurry_tank.description: A circular building built to hold a liquid mix of primarily animal excreta (also known as slurry).

sports_centre.description: A building that was built as a sports centre.

sports_hall.description: A building that was built as a sports hall.

stable.description: A building constructed as a stable for horses.

stadium.description: A building constructed to be a stadium building, including buildings that are abandoned and used nowadays for a different purpose.

static_caravan.description: A mobile home (semi)permanently left on a single site

stilt_house.description: A building raised on piles over the surface of the soil or a body of water

storage_tank.description: Storage tanks are containers that hold liquids

sty.description: Asty(pigsty, pig ark, pig-shed) is a building for raising domestic pigs, usually found on farms.

supermarket.description: A building constructed to house a self-service large-area store.

synagogue.description: A building that was built as a synagogue. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the grounds where it is in current use.

tech_cab.description: This article or section containsquestionable, contentiousorcontroversial information. See thetalk pagefor more information.Overlaps with predominatebuilding=service+building:prefabricated=yesSmall prefabricated cabin structures for the air-conditioned accommodation of different technology.

temple.description: A building that was built as a temple. Used in conjunction withamenity=place_of_worship,religion=*,denomination=*andlanduse=religiousfor the grounds where it is in current use.

tent.description: For a permanently placed tent.Do not map tents placed temporarily.

terrace.description: A single way used to define the outline of a linear row of residential dwellings, each of which normally has its own entrance, which form a terrace ("row-house" or "townhouse" in North American English). Consider defining each dwelling separately using 'house'.

tower.description: A tower-building. Best combined withtower:type=*. See alsoman_made=towerfor a tag with a similar definition.

train_station.description: A building constructed to be a train station building, including buildings that are abandoned and used nowadays for a different purpose.

transformer_tower.description: A transformer tower is a characteristic tall building comprising a distribution transformer and constructed to connect directly  to a medium voltage overhead power line. Quite often the power line has since been undergrounded but the building may still serve as a substation. If the building is still in use as a substation it should additionally be tagged aspower=substation+substation=minor_distribution.

transportation.description: A building related to public transport. You will probably want to tag it with proper transport related tag as well, such aspublic_transport=station. Note that there is a special tag for train station buildings -building=train_station.

tree_house.description: An accommodation, often designed as a small hut, sometimes also as a room or small apartment. Built on tree posts or on a natural tree. A tree house has no contact with the ground. Access via ladders, stairs or bridgeways.

triumphal_arch.description: A free-standing monumental structure in the shape of an archway with one or more arched passageways.

trullo.description: A stone hut with a conical roof.

university.description: A university building. Useamenity=universityfor the university as an institution.

user defined.description: All commonly used valuesaccording toTaginfo, generallybuilding types

warehouse.description: A building primarily intended for the storage or goods or as part of a distribution system.

windmill.description: A building constructed as a traditional windmill, historically used to mill grain with wind power.

[en].description: Healthcare counselling, e.g. an addiction centre, a nutritionist, or a sex therapist. Also seehealthcare=nutrition_counselling

agricultural_engines.description: A workplace or office of a person who plans and construct engines and tools for agricultural use.

atelier.description: A place where visual artists work

bag_repair.description: A place where bags/luggage are repaired.

bakery.description: A workplace for fresh bakery goods. May have no shop or be combined with a shop.

blacksmith.description: A place where a blacksmith forges tools, horseshoes, etc. from iron.

boatbuilder.description: A workplace where boats are planned and constructed

bookbinder.description: A workplace for physically assembling a book from a number of sheets of paper.

brewery.description: A dedicated building for the making of beer.

builder.description: A workplace or office of a tradesman who performs structural alterations and additions to buildings.

cabinet_maker.description: A person who makes fine wooden furniture

candlemaker.description: A person who makes candles or a manufactory where candles were made.

car_painter.description: A place specializing in painting cars.

carpenter.description: A workplace or office of carpenters that work with timber to construct, install and maintain buildings, furniture, and other objects.

carpet_cleaner.description: A carpet cleaner is a tradesman who specializes in cleaning carpets

carpet_layer.description: A carpet layer is a tradesmen who specializes in laying carpets

caterer.description: A workplace or office of one who prepares customized meals for takeout, or provides prepared meals or supplies to a group at social gatherings

chimney_sweeper.description: A workplace or office of a person who cleans chimneys for a living.

cleaning.description: Workplace or office of a person who cleans buildings, industrial facilities or windows.

clockmaker.description: A workplace or office of a clockmaker that is building, repairing or engraving clocks of all sorts.

clothes_mending.description: A place where clothing is mended (repaired) or altered professionally.

confectionery.description: A place where the set of food items that are rich in sugar, any one or type of which is called a confection is produced.

cooper.description: A person or company that manufacture of containers and vessels mainly made of wood.

dental_technician.description: A workplace for a dental technician who constructs custom-made restorative and dental appliances.

distillery.description: An establishment for distilling, especially for distilling alcoholic liquors.

door_construction.description: A workplace of someone constructing doors

dressmaker.description: Dressmaker is a person who makes custom clothing for women, such as dresses, blouses, and evening gowns.

electrician.description: A person or company that works with electricity or electrical systems.

electronics_repair.description: Repair shops and service centres which provides repair of computers, phones, appliance etc.

engraver.description: A workplace or office of an engraver.

fence_maker.description: A workplace or office of a person or company specialized in manufacturing and installing fences.

floorer.description: A workplace or office of a floorer.

gardener.description: A workplace or office of a garden designer or a landscape gardener.

glassblower.description: A person or company that blows bottles or other objects from molten glass.

glaziery.description: A place where residential, commercial, and artistic glass is selected, cut, installed, replaced, and removed.

goldsmith.description: The workplace of a person who forges things out of gold, especially jewelry.

grinding_mill.description: A device that breaks solid materials into smaller pieces by grinding, crushing, or cutting. The aftermath is powdered product, not liquid extract like oil mill.

gunsmith.description: A workplace of someone who repairs or modifies firearms.

handicraft.description: A place where useful and decorative devices are made completely by hand or using only simple tools

hvac.description: A workplace or office of a person or company that install and maintain HVAC systems (Heating, Ventilating, and Air Conditioning).

insulation.description: A workplace or office of a person who does thermal insulation in buildings.

interior_decorator.description: Workplace or office of a person who does interior decoratoring.

interior_work.description: Workplace of a person who builds/installs non-load-bearing interior walls, especially drywall.

jeweller.description: A place where necklaces, rings, brooches, earrings and bracelets and other personal adornments are created or repaired.

joiner.description: An artisan who builds things by joining pieces of wood, particularly furniture or ornamental work.

key_cutter.description: A place where keys can be duplicated from originals.

leather.description: A leatherworker; a person who makes things from leather.

locksmith.description: The workshop of a craftsman who installs or repairs locks and opens locked doors.

luthier.description: Workshop of a luthier, who makes or repairs stringed wooden musical instruments, such as lutes, violins, and guitars.

metal_construction.description: Workplace or office of a person who work with metal. This applies to: planning, construction, trade, repair.

mint.description: A place where coins and medals are hand crafted.

oil_mill.description: A mill designed to crush or bruise oil-bearing seeds, such as linseed or peanuts, or other oil-rich vegetable material.

optician.description: A place where lenses for the correction of a person's vision are designed, fitted and dispensed.

organ_builder.description: Organ building is the profession of designing, building, restoring and maintaining pipe organs.

painter.description: A workplace or office of a house painter, which is a tradesman responsible for the painting and decorating of buildings.

parquet_layer.description: A workplace or office of a parquet layer.

paver.description: The workshop or office of a paver.

pest_control.description: A business that offers pest control services

photographer.description: A workplace or office of a person who takes photographs using a camera.

photographic_laboratory.description: A place where photos taken on a film or digital images are transformed into a more permanent form, often on paper or plastic foil.

photovoltaic.description: A workplace or office who installed or planned photovoltaic systems.

piano_tuner.description: A piano tuner or technician. A professional that tunes, regulates and makes the maintenance of pianos. Most of them repair simple problems and some restore a whole piano.

plasterer.description: A workplace or office of a tradesman who works with plaster, such as forming a layer of plaster on an interior wall or plaster decorative moldings on ceilings or walls.

plumber.description: A workplace or office of a skilled tradesperson who specializes in (drinking) water supply, sewage and drainage systems.

pottery.description: A place where earthenware, stoneware and porcelain and other ceramic ware is made by potters.

printer.description: A small printing business which produces published works such as newspapers, books, magazines, etc.

rigger.description: The workplace of a person who sets up the rigging for sailboats and sailing ships (or possibly a person who works on ropes, booms, lifts, hoists and the like for a stage production).

roofer.description: A workplace or office of a tradesman who is specialized in roof covering and sealing.

saddler.description: A place where saddles or accessories for cars and boats are produced or repaired.

sailmaker.description: A workshop of a sailmaker who cleans, repairs and makes sails.

sawmill.description: A sawmill is a facility where logs are cut into timber

scaffolder.description: A workplace or office of a tradesman who builds temporary structure used to support people and material in the construction or repair of buildings and other large structures.

shoemaker.description: A place where shoes, boots, sandals, clogs and moccasins are created, repaired or altered to fit personal needs.

signmaker.description: A maker of signs, mainly for shops and other businesses.

stand_builder.description: A workplace of a company that builds stands/booths for fairs.

stonemason.description: A place where rough pieces of rock are shaped into accurate geometrical shapes.

stove_fitter.description: A workplace/office of a person / company who designs and builds stoves / tiled stoves.

sun_protection.description: A place where sun protection is made, repaired, or delivered for assembly, especially awnings and shutters.

tailor.description: A place where clothing is made, repaired, or altered professionally, especially suits and men's clothing.

tatami.description: A workplace or office of an artisan for tatami mat.

tiler.description: A workplace or office of a person who lays tiles on floors, swimming pools and such.

tinsmith.description: A person who makes and repairs things made of light-coloured metal, particularly tinware.

toolmaker.description: A workplace of a company that builds complex tools, machine tools, moulds, gauges,... .

turner.description: A workplace or office of a turner.

upholsterer.description: A place where furniture, especially seats are provided with padding, springs, webbing, and fabric or leather covers.

watchmaker.description: A watchmaker is an artisan who makes and repairs watches. Today due to industrial production they mostly repair watches.

water_well_drilling.description: A craftsman or company who drill water wells.

weaver.description: Workshop of a person producing something by weaving, may offer products for sale

welder.description: One who unites pieces of metal with high temperature.

window_construction.description: A workplace of someone constructing windows.

[en].description: A clinic for day patients to get a haemodialysisDialysis

ambulance_station.description: An ambulance station is a structure or other area set aside for storage of ambulance vehicles, medical equipment, personal protective equipment, and other medical supplies.

assembly_point.description: A designated (safe) place where people can gather or must report to during an emergency or a fire drill.

defibrillator.description: Defibrillator (AED), an external and portable electronic device that diagnoses and can correct arrhythmia of the heart automatically.

drinking_water.description: A facility that provides drinking water in emergency situations

emergency_ward_entrance.description: The entrance to an emergency ward.

fire_alarm_box.description: A device used for notifying a fire department of a fire.

fire_extinguisher.description: An active fire protection device used to extinguish or control small fires, often in emergency situations.

fire_hose.description: A high-pressure hose used to carry water or other fire retardant (such as foam) to a fire to extinguish it.

fire_hydrant.description: An active fire protection measure, and a source of water provided in most urban, suburban, and rural areas with municipal water service to enable fire fighters to tap into the municipal water supply to assist in extinguishing a fire.

fire_service_inlet.description: An inlet that allows the fire brigade to pump water into a building.

landing_site.description: Preselected flat area for a helicopter to land in an emergency situation

life_ring.description: A floating ring to throw out to someone who is struggling in water.

lifeguard.description: A place where a lifeguard is on duty

phone.description: A calling device can be used to tell on your current position in case of an emergency. Use withref=NUMBER_ON_THE_SIGN

siren.description: A loud noise maker, such as an air raid siren or a tornado siren.

suction_point.description: A preferred point to pump water off a river or other waters for a fire department.

water_tank.description: A large water basin or tank for a fire department to take water.

cone.description: A landform with a distinctly conical shape

dyke.description: a sheet of rock that fills a fracture of a pre-existing rock body or when standing alone looking like a man made wall

fault.description: A geological fault is a planar fracture or discontinuity in a volume of rock.

fold.description: Planar surfaces, such as sedimentary strata, that are bent or curved ("folded")

giants_kettle.description: A regular hole in a rock created by the rotation of stones in the bed of a stream.

glacial_erratic.description: A boulder deposited by a glacier.

inselberg.description: Inselberg are isolated rock hill, knob, ridge, or small mountain

limestone_pavement.description: A natural karst landform consisting of a flat surface of limestone

meteor_crater.description: A crater formed by the impact of a meteor.

monocline.description: Step-like fold in rock strata consisting of a zone of a dip comprised between ~65° and 90°

moraine.description: Any accumulation of unconsolidated rock debris previously carried by a glacier.

outcrop.description: A place where the bedrock or superficial deposits previously covered under the soil have become locally exposed

rock_glacier.description: Rock glaciers are mixtures of rock and ice that move slowly downhill when active

sinkhole.description: A depression or hole in the ground caused by some form of collapse of the surface

tor.description: A large, free-standing rock outcrop that rises abruptly

unconformity.description: A buried erosional surface separating two rock masses or strata of different ages

volcanic_lava_field.description: An area with volcanic lava on the ground.

volcanic_vent.description: A hole through which the lava erupts.

[en].description: Someone practisingalternative or complementary medicinebut is not amedical practitioneror a place where alternative or complementary medicine is practices that does not fit into the other categories (excepthealthcare=yes)

bridleway.description: For horse riders. Pedestrians are usually also permitted, cyclists may be permitted depending on local rules/laws. Motor vehicles are forbidden.

bus_stop.description: A small bus stop. Optionally one may also usepublic_transport=stop_positionfor the position where the vehicle stops andpublic_transport=platformfor the place where passengers wait.

footway.description: For designated footpaths; i.e., mainly/exclusively for pedestrians. This includes walking tracks and gravel paths. If bicycles are allowed as well, you can indicate this by adding a

hitchhiking.description: Like a bus_stop but for free, uncertain, spontaneous rides with strangers.

bus_guideway.description: A busway where the vehicle guided by the way (though not a railway) and is not suitable for other traffic. Please note: this is not a normal bus lane, useaccess=no,psv=yesinstead! If the buses are not guided, considerhighway=busway.

busway.description: A dedicated roadway for bus rapid transit systems

corridor.description: For a hallway inside of a building.

crossing.description: A.k.a. crosswalk. Pedestrians can cross a street here; e.g., zebra crossing

cyclist_waiting_aid.description: Street furniture for cyclists that are intended to make waiting at esp. traffic lights more comfortable.

elevator.description: An elevator or lift, used to travel vertically, providing passenger and freight access between pathways at different floor levels.

emergency_access_point.description: Sign number which can be used to define your current position in case of an emergency. Use withref=NUMBER_ON_THE_SIGN. See alsoemergency=access_point

emergency_bay.description: An area beside a highway where you can safely stop your car in case of breakdown or emergency.

escape.description: For runaway truck ramps, runaway truck lanes, emergency escape ramps, or truck arrester beds. It enables vehicles with braking failure to safely stop.

ladder.description: A vertical or inclined set of steps or rungs intended for climbing or descending of a person with the help of hands.

living_street.description: Forliving streets, which are residential streets where pedestrians have legal priority over cars, speeds are kept very low.

mini_roundabout.description: Similar to roundabouts, but at the center there is either a painted circle or a fullytraversableisland. In case of an untraversable center island,junction=roundaboutshould be used.Rendered as anti-clockwise by defaultdirection=anticlockwise. To render clockwise add the tagdirection=clockwise.

motorway_junction.description: Indicates a junction (UK) or exit (US).ref=*should be set to the exit number or junction identifier. (Some roads – e.g., the A14 – also carry junction numbers, so the tag may be encountered elsewhere despite its name)

motorway_link.description: The link roads (sliproads/ramps) leading to/from a motorway from/to a motorway or lower class highway. Normally with the same motorway restrictions.

passing_place.description: The location of a passing space

pedestrian.description: For roads used mainly/exclusively for pedestrians in shopping and some residential areas which may allow access by motorised vehicles only for very limited periods of the day. To create a 'square' or 'plaza' create a closed way and tag as pedestrian and also witharea=yes.

platform.description: A platform at a bus stop or station.

primary_link.description: The link roads (sliproads/ramps) leading to/from a primary road from/to a primary road or lower class highway.

raceway.description: A course or track for (motor) racing

rest_area.description: Place where drivers can leave the road to rest, but not refuel.

road.description: A road/way/street/motorway/etc. ofunknowntype. It can stand for anything ranging from a footpath to a motorway. This tag should only be used temporarily until the road/way/etc. has been properly surveyed. If youdoknow the road type, donotuse this value, instead use one of the more specifichighway=*values.

secondary_link.description: The link roads (sliproads/ramps) leading to/from a secondary road from/to a secondary road or lower class highway.

service.description: For access roads to, or within an industrial estate, camp site, business park, car park, alleys, etc. Can be used in conjunction withservice=*to indicate the type of usage and withaccess=*to indicate who can use it and in what circumstances.

services.description: A service station to get food and eat something, often found at motorways

speed_camera.description: A fixed road-side or overhead speed camera.

speed_display.description: A dynamic electronic sign that displays the speed of the passer-by motorist.

steps.description: For flights of steps (stairs) on footways. Use withstep_count=*to indicate the number of steps

street_lamp.description: A street light, lamppost, street lamp, light standard, or lamp standard is a raised source of light on the edge of a road, which is turned on or lit at a certain time every night

tertiary_link.description: The link roads (sliproads/ramps) leading to/from a tertiary road from/to a tertiary road or lower class highway.

toll_gantry.description: A toll gantry is a gantry suspended over a way, usually a motorway, as part of a system of electronic toll collection. For a toll booth with any kind of barrier or booth see:barrier=toll_booth

traffic_mirror.description: Mirror that reflects the traffic on one road when direct view is blocked.

trailhead.description: Designated place to start on a trail or route

trunk_link.description: The link roads (sliproads/ramps) leading to/from a trunk road from/to a trunk road or lower class highway.

turning_circle.description: A turning circle is a rounded, widened area usually, but not necessarily, at the end of a road to facilitate easier turning of a vehicle. Also known as acul de sac.

turning_loop.description: A widened area of a highway with a non-traversable island for turning around, often circular and at the end of a road.

via_ferrata.description: A via ferrata is a route equipped with fixed cables, stemples, ladders, and bridges in order to increase ease and security for climbers. These via ferrata require equipment : climbing harness, shock absorber and two short lengths of rope, but do not require a long rope as for climbing.

aircraft.description: A decommissioned aircraft which generally remains in one place.

anchor.description: A historic/retired anchor. Usually found in historic maritime areas.

aqueduct.description: A historic structure to convey water

archaeological_site.description: A place in which evidence of past activity is preserved.

battlefield.description: The site of a battle or military skirmish in the past.

boundary_stone.description: A historic physical marker that identifies a historic boundary

bullaun_stone.description: A stone with one or more depressions

cannon.description: A historic/retired cannon. Usually found on forts or battlefields.

caravanserai.description: Inn that provided lodging for caravans

castle.description: Used for various kinds of castles, palaces, fortresses, manors, stately homes, kremlins, shiros and other.

castle_wall.description: A fortification surrounding the bailey of a castle.

cattle_crush.description: A cattle crush is a strongly built stall for holding cattle.

charcoal_pile.description: Historic site of a charcoal pile. Often still in good condition in hilly forest areas.

church.description: A church with a historical value.

city_gate.description: A city gate within a city wall.

citywalls.description: A citywall is a fortification used to defend a city.

creamery.description: A creamery is an industrial building where butter and sometimes cheese or ice-cream were made from milk. For rural communities, it also served as a social gathering point.

epigraph.description: A historic inscription on an object

farm.description: A historical farm, kept in its original state.

fort.description: A military fort, a stand-alone defensive structure which differs from a castle in that there is no permanent residence.

high_cross.description: An early medieaval standing cross, richly decorated, often with a ring surrounding the crossing point.

highwater_mark.description: A marker indicating a past flood or high water.

lavoir.description: A disused lavoir mapped for its historical value.

lime_kiln.description: Built structure which was used in the past to produce quicklime from limestone.

locomotive.description: A decommissioned locomotive which generally remains in one place

manor.description: Historic manors/mansions having different use today

memorial.description: Small memorials, usually remembering special persons, people who lost their lives in the wars, past events or missing places.

milestone.description: A historic marker that shows the distance to important destinations.

millstone.description: A large round stone used for grinding grain or other materials.

mine.description: Location of historic underground mine workings for minerals such as coal or lead.

minecart.description: A cart used to transport coal or ore from a mine.

monastery.description: Building/place that is a historically significant monastery.

monument.description: A memorial object, which is especially large, built to remember, show respect to a person or group of people or to commemorate an event.

mosque.description: A mosque with a historical and archaeological value.

ogham_stone.description: A stone with an Ogham script on it. They are most commonly found in Ireland as free standing stones, lying on the ground, recycled in buildings such as churches or as artefacts in museums.

pound.description: A former amenity to hold stray or seized animals, often walled.

railway_car.description: A decommissioned railway car which generally remains in one place

road.description: A road or street that is of historical importance.

round_tower.description: Slim, conical tower built as a bell tower and watchtower in Ireland.

ruins.description: Remains of structures that were once complete, but have fallen into partial or complete disrepair.

rune_stone.description: Stones, boulders or bedrock with historical runic inscriptions.

ship.description: A decommissioned ship/submarine which generally remains in one place

stećak.description: Specifies megalithic gravestone from medieval Bosnia.

stone.description: A stone shaped or placed by man with historical value.

tank.description: A decommissioned tank which generally remains in one place

temple.description: An ancient pagan temple in_situ and in various degree of preservation, such as Ziggurat, Egyptian temple, Mithraeum, Erechtheion, Buddhist, Meso-American and other ancient religious and rituals' temples, with a historical and archaeological value.

tomb.description: A structure where somebody has been buried.

tower.description: This property distinguishes a tower as historic

vehicle.description: A decommissioned vehicle which generally remains in one place

warehouse.description: A historic warehouse, a building to store bulk produce or goods for commercial purposes.

wayside_cross.description: A historical cross along a way, symbol of Christian faith.

wayside_shrine.description: A shrine often showing a religious depiction. Tag is used also for modern shrines.

wreck.description: A nautical craft that has been sunk or destroyed.

yes.description: Used to add the historic significance of the objects described by other tags.

[en].description: A Hospice which provides palliative care to terminal ill people and support to their relatives.
Medical laboratoryalso called (analytical, diagnostic) is a place that analyses body fluids such as blood, urine, faeces etc .

aquaculture.description: Aquacultureis the farming of freshwater and saltwater organisms such as finfish, molluscs, crustaceans and aquatic plants.Warning: currently, there is no convention on the exact meaning of this tag. Therefore, it makes sense to treat it like "boundary of aquaculture" (without implication of water bo

basin.description: An area artificially graded to hold water.Together withbasin=*for stormwater/rainwater infiltration/detention/retention basins.Other languages.

brownfield.description: Describes land scheduled for new development where old buildings have been demolished and cleared

commercial.description: Predominantly commercial businesses and their offices. Commercial businesses which sell goods should be categorised aslanduse=retail. Commercial businesses can sell services on site and may include private Doctor's Surgeries, and those non-government services for mental and physical health, such as

education.description: An area predominately used for educational purposes/facilities.

farmland.description: An area of farmland used for tillage (cereals, vegetables, oil plants, flowers).

grass.description: An area of mown and managed grass not otherwise covered by a more specific tag. Some view this as not a landuse, see the main page

greenery.description: Used for any area covered with landscaping or decorative greenery, regardless of it being on a roundabout, along a street or in a park/garden, This tag has been used for vegetation that is hard to classify, either because its kind or because its diversity.

greenfield.description: Describes land scheduled for new development where there have been no buildings before. A greenfield is scheduled to turn into a construction site

logging.description: An area where some or all trees have been cut down

meadow.description: A meadow or pasture: land primarily vegetated by grass and non-woody plants, mainly used for hay or grazing

orchard.description: intentional planting of trees or shrubs maintained for food production

railway.description: Ground used around railways and railwaystations.

recreation_ground.description: An open green space for general recreation, which may include pitches, nets and so on, usually municipal but possibly also private to colleges or companies

village_green.description: A village green is a distinctive area of grassy public land in a village centre. Not a generic tag for urban greenery. It is a typical English term – defined separately from 'common land' under the Commons Registration Act 1965 and the Commons Act 2006.

winter_sports.description: An area dedicated to winter sports (e.g. skiing)

allotments.description: A piece of land given over to local residents for growing vegetables and flowers.

animal_keeping.description: An area of land that is used to keep animals, particularly horses and livestock.

cemetery.description: Place for burials. You can addreligion=*(values listed in theplace of worship page). Smaller places (e.g. with a church nearby) may useamenity=grave_yardinstead.

conservation.description: Protected areas (deprecated) Alternate tagging of same thing:boundary=protected_area,protected_area=*

construction.description: A site which is under active development and construction of a building or structure, including any purposeful alteration to the land or vegetation upon it. Abandoned construction projects and sites should not use this tag.

depot.description: An area used as a depot for e.g. vehicles (trains, buses or trams). Consider using rather standard landuse tags likelanduse=railway,landuse=commercial,landuse=industrial, with subtag likeindustrial=depot.

farmyard.description: An area of land with farm buildings like farmhouse, dwellings, farmsteads, sheds, stables, barns, equipment sheds, feed bunkers, etc. plus the open space in between them and the shrubbery/trees around them.

garages.description: One level buildings with boxes commonly for cars, usually made of brick and metal. Usually this area belong to garage cooperative with own name, chairman, budget, rules, security, etc.

greenhouse_horticulture.description: Area used for growing plants in greenhouses

highway.description: Area of land used for a highway, including all auxiliary areas like associated footways, verges and ditches.

industrial.description: Predominantly industrial landuses such as workshops, factories, or warehouses.

institutional.description: Land used for institutional purposes, seeInstitution (disambiguation). Institutional land may contain one or more buildings like anoffice=government,office=ngo,office=association,amenity=social_facilityand their associated infrastructure (car parks, service roads, yards and so-on) inside the area.The meaning of this tag is ambiguous and unclear by some. Consider using another tag that better describes the object. For instancelanduse=civic_admin.

military.description: For land areas owned/used by the military for whatever purpose

plant_nursery.description: intentional planting of plants maintaining for the production of new plants

port.description: coastal industrial area where commercial traffic is handled. Consider usinglanduse=industrial+industrial=portfor this type of industrial area.

religious.description: An area used for religious purposes

reservoir.description: Areservoiron Wikipedia. Deprecated variant ofnatural=water+water=reservoir. SeeProposed features/Reservoirfor details.

residential.description: Land where people reside; predominantly residential detached (single houses, grouped dwellings), or attached (apartments, flats, units) dwellings. For "Mixed-Use" areas where more than half of the land is residential, tag as residential.See also:Land use and areas of natural land § Open questions

retail.description: Predominantly retail businesses such as shops. Retail businesses sell physical goods such as food (prepared or grocery), clothing, medicine, stationary, appliances, tools, or other similar physical items. Retail businesses have high amounts of public foot traffic. Retail businesses do not exclusively provide or sell their services. For businesses which sell services seelanduse=commercial.Generally a member of the public could freely walk into and access a retail business. Retail is a subset of commercial, but should be used and treated as mutually exclusive in OpenStreetMap.

salt_pond.description: A place where salt (saline) water is evaporated to extract its salt.

vineyard.description: A piece of land where grapes are grown.

adult_gaming_centre.description: A venue with gambling machines, usually with a minimum age requirement.

firepit.description: A fire ring or fire pit, often at a campsite or picnic site.

garden.description: A place where flowers and other plants are grown in a decorative and structured manner or for scientific purposes.

nature_reserve.description: A protected area of importance for wildlife, flora, fauna or features of geological or other special interest.

park.description: A park, usually in an urban (municipal) setting, created for recreation and relaxation.

summer_camp.description: A place for supervised camps for children or teenagers conducted during the summer months.

bandstand.description: A bandstand is an open structure where musical bands can perform concerts.

beach_resort.description: A managed beach, including within the boundary any associated facilities. Entry may also require payment of a fee.

bird_hide.description: A place that is used to observe wildlife, especially birds.

common.description: Identify land over which the public has general rights of use for certain leisure activities.

dance.description: A dance venue or dance hall.

disc_golf_course.description: A place to play disc golf.

dog_park.description: A designated area, with or without a fenced boundary, where dog-owners are permitted to exercise their pets unrestrained.

escape_game.description: A physical adventure game in which players solve a series of puzzles using clues, hints and strategy to complete the objectives at hand.

fishing.description: A public or private place for fishing.

fitness_centre.description: Fitness centre, health club or gym with exercise machines, fitness classes or both, for exercise.

fitness_station.description: An outdoor facility where people can practise typical fitness exercises.

hackerspace.description: A place where people with common interests (science, technology, ...) meet.

horse_riding.description: A facility where people practise horse riding, usually in their spare time, e.g. a riding centre. For a riding arena useleisure=pitch+sport=equestrian.

ice_rink.description: A place where you can skate and play bandy or ice hockey.

marina.description: A facility for mooring leisure yachts and motor boats.

miniature_golf.description: A place or area where you can play miniature golf.

paddling_pool[en].description: A paddling pool is a shallow pool, typically less than 30cm (1 ft.) deep, for children to play in.

picnic_table.description: A table with benches for food and rest.

pitch.description: An area designed for practising a particular sport, normally designated with appropriate markings.

playground.description: A playground: an area designed for children to play.

slipway.description: A slipway: a ramp for launching a boat into water.

sports_centre.description: A sports centre is a distinct facility where sports take place within an enclosed area.

stadium.description: A major sports facility with substantial tiered seating.

swimming_area.description: An area for swimming within a larger body of water (such as a river, lake or the sea) that is marked by a rope, buoys or similar.

track.description: A track for running, cycling and other non-motorised racing such as horses, greyhounds.

water_park.description: An amusement park with features like water slides, recreational pools (e.g. wave pools) or lazy rivers.

tower.description: A tower used by lifeguard to watch and supervise swimmers in order to prevent drownings and other dangers.

bay.description: A power line within a substation which connects a circuit to a busbar.

busbar.description: Identifies a power line as a busbar, the central connection in a substation

adit.description: A type of entrance to an underground mine which is horizontal or nearly horizontal.

beacon.description: A structure for signalling on land and sea

breakwater.description: A man-made structure designed to protect a shore or harbour from the sea and waves.

bridge.description: The outline of a bridge, grouping together all features for that bridge.

bunker_silo.description: An open-sided structure without a roof that can be used with vehicles to fill and empty them

carpet_hanger.description: A construction to hang carpets for cleaning with the help of carpet beaters.

chimney.description: A tall distinctive vertical conduit for venting hot gases or smoke, normally found near power stations or large factories.

clearcut.description: A clear-cut area where most of the trees have been felled.

column.description: A column used to support a structure or for decoration.

communications_tower.description: A huge tower for transmitting radio applications

cross.description: Cross, especially one with little historical value

cutline.description: A straight line cut in a forest.

dovecote.description: A place where doves are farmed or stored.

dyke.description: An embankment built to restrict the flow of water or other liquids

embankment.description: An artificial steep slope. Lower side to right (arrows side).

flagpole.description: A long pole built to hold a flag

gasometer.description: A large container in which natural gas or town gas is stored near atmospheric pressure at ambient temperatures

goods_conveyor.description: A conveyor system for transporting materials

groyne.description: A rigid structure built from a shoreline or riverbank that interrupts water flow and limits the movement of sediment.

guard_stone.description: A guard stone: a stone built onto or into the corner of a building or wall to prevent carriages from damaging the structure, often found on either side of an entrance to a laneway, or alongside a wall to protect it.

kiln.description: A thermally insulated chamber used for processes such as burning, hardening, drying, or smelting.

lighthouse.description: Tower that emits light to serve as a navigational aid at sea or on inland waterway

mast.description: A mast is a vertical structure built to hold, for example, antennas.

mineshaft.description: A vertical tunnel into a mine where minerals are extracted.

obelisk.description: Tall, narrow, four-sided, tapered monument which usually ends in a pyramid-like shape at the top.

observatory.description: Observatory: a location used for observing terrestrial or celestial events.

offshore_platform.description: Offshore platform, oil platform or offshore drilling rig

petroleum_well.description: A hole bored in the earth, designed to bring petroleum oil or gas to the surface

pier.description: Raised walkway over water supported by pillars or floating and secured.

pipeline.description: A major pipeline carrying gas, water, oil, etc.

pump.description: A device in charge of moving or raising the level of liquids.

pumping_station.description: Pumping station: a facility including pumps and equipment for pumping fluids from one place to another

reservoir_covered.description: A covered reservoir is a large man-made tank for holding fresh water

silo.description: A storage container for bulk material, often grains such as corn or wheat.

snow_fence.description: A fence-like structure built to redirect drifting snow.

snow_net.description: A netting fence built across steep slopes to reduce risk and severity of (snow) avalanches

storage_tank.description: A container that holds liquids or compressed gases.

street_cabinet.description: A cabinet located in the street and hosting technical equipment to operate facilities such as electricity or street lights.

stupa.description: A Buddhist dome-shaped structure with a spire on top.

surveillance.description: A surveillance camera or other type of surveillance equipment.

survey_point.description: A triangulation pillar, geodetic vertex, or other piece of fixed equipment used by topographers.
