tailings_pond.description: A body of liquid or slurry used to store byproducts of mining operations.

telescope.description: Telescope: an instrument that aids in the observation of remote objects by collecting light or radio waves

tower.description: A tower is a free-standing structure which is higher than it is wide.

video_wall.description: A digital screen, typically constructed out of smaller LED panels.

wastewater_plant.description: A wastewater plant is a facility used to treat wastewater.

water_tower.description: Structure with a water tank at an altitude to increase pressure in water network

water_well.description: A structural facility to access ground water, created by digging or drilling.

water_works.description: A facility where water is treated to make it suitable for human consumption.

wildlife_crossing.description: Structure that allow animals to cross human barriers safely.

windmill.description: A traditional windmill, historically used to mill grain with wind power.

works.description: A factory or industrial production plant.

[en].description: AMidwife, a health professional who cares for mothers and newborns around childbirth.

academy.description: A training establishment for military service members

airfield.description: A place where military planes take off and land.

barracks.description: Buildings where military personnel live and sleep.

base.description: A facility where military personnel and equipment are based.

bunker.description: A military installation capable of withstanding an attack, which is currently used as a bunker.

checkpoint.description: Place where civilian visitors and vehicles will be checked by a military authority.

danger_area.description: A danger area is a restricted area posing a threat to life or property.

obstacle_course.description: A military obstacle course: a series of challenging physical obstructions an individual or team must navigate usually while being timed.

office.description: Military offices, e.g. general staff office, military recruitment office etc

range.description: Where military personnel practice with their weapons (firing, bombing, artillery, ...).

school.description: A school for children run as part of a country's military

trench.description: A military trench is an excavation in the ground that is generally deeper than it is wide, dug into the ground as a barrier for military purposes (e.g. trench warfare).

arch.description: A rock arch naturally formed by erosion, with an opening underneath.

arete.description: A thin, almost knife-like, ridge of rock which is typically formed when two glaciers erode parallel U-shaped valleys

bare_rock.description: An area with sparse or no vegetation, so that the bedrock becomes visible

bay.description: Named area of water mostly surrounded by land but with level connection to the ocean or a lake.

beach.description: Landform along a body of water which consists of sand, shingle or other loose material.

blockfield.description: An area covered by boulder- or block-sized rocks.

blowhole.description: An opening to a sea cave which has grown landwards resulting in blasts of water from the opening due to the wave action.

cape.description: A piece of elevated land sticking out into the sea or large lake.

cave_entrance.description: An entrance to a cave: a natural underground space large enough for a human to enter

cliff.description: A vertical or almost vertical natural drop in terrain, usually with a bare rock surface. The bottom of the cliff is on the right side of the way.

coastline.description: The line between sea and land indicating mean high water (springs): the water lies on the right side of the way.

crevasse.description: A large crack in a glacier

dune.description: A hill of sand formed by wind, covered with no or very little vegetation

earth_bank.description: Large erosion gully or steep earth bank.

fell.description: Habitat above tree line covered with grass, dwarf shrubs and mosses.

fumarole.description: A fumarole is an opening in a planet's crust, which emits steam and gases

geyser.description: A spring characterized by intermittent discharge of water ejected turbulently and accompanied by steam

glacier.description: A permanent body of ice formed naturally from snow that is moving under its own weight.

grassland.description: An area where the vegetation is dominated by grasses and other herbaceous (non-woody) plants.

heath.description: A dwarf-shrub habitat, characterised by open, low growing woody vegetation, often dominated by plants of the Ericaceae.

hill.description: A hill is a landform that is elevated above the surrounding terrain, but is smaller than a mountain.

hot_spring.description: A spring of geothermally heated groundwater

isthmus.description: A narrow strip of land, bordered by water on both sides and connecting two larger land masses

mud.description: Area covered with mud: water saturated fine grained soil without significant plant growth.

peak.description: The top (summit) of a hill or mountain.

peninsula.description: A piece of land projecting into water from a larger land mass, nearly surrounded by water

reef.description: A feature (rock, sandbar, coral, etc) lying permanently beneath the surface of the water.

ridge.description: A mountain landform with a continuous elevated crest or linear hill

rock.description: A notable rock or group of rocks attached to the underlying bedrock

saddle.description: The lowest point along a ridge or between two mountain tops.

sand.description: An area covered by sand with no or very little vegetation.

scree.description: Unconsolidated angular stones formed by rockfall and weathering from adjacent rock faces.

scrub.description: Uncultivated land covered with shrubs, bushes or stunted trees.

shingle.description: An accumulation of rounded rock fragments on a beach or riverbed.

shoal.description: An area of the water floor which nears the water surface and is exposed at low tide or when a river/lake is not full of water.

shrubbery.description: An area of woody shrubbery that is actively maintained or pruned by humans. A slightly wilder look is also possible.

sinkhole.description: A natural depression or hole in the surface topography.

spring.description: A place where ground water flows naturally from the ground

stone.description: A single notable freestanding rock, which may differ from the composition of the terrain it lies in.

strait.description: A narrow area of water surrounded by land on two sides and by water on two other sides.

tundra.description: Treeless cold climate habitat with open, low growing sedges, grasses, mosses and lichens.

valley.description: A natural depression flanked by ridges or ranges of mountains or hills

volcano.description: An opening exposed on the earth's surface where volcanic material is emitted.

water.description: Any inland body of water, from natural such as a lake or pond to artificial like a moat or canal.

wetland.description: A natural area subject to inundation or with waterlogged ground

[en].description: Nursing. A facility where some nurses may receive their patients.
Someone who practicesoccupational therapybut is not amedical practitioner.

advertising_agency.description: A service-based business dedicated to creating, planning, and handling advertising.

airline.description: An office for an airline company.

architect.description: An office for an architect or group of architects.

association.description: An office of a non-profit organisation, society, e.g. student, sport, consumer, automobile, bike association, etc.

broadcaster.description: A radio or television broadcasting station.

chamber.description: Seat of a professional chamber, such as bar associations or chambers of commerce.

charity.description: An office of a charitable organization

company.description: An office of a private company.

construction_company.description: An office of a building construction company.

consulting.description: An office for a consulting firm, providing expert professional advice to other companies or organisations.

courier.description: A courier delivery service, which is neither a post office nor a national post.

coworking.description: An office where people can go to work (typically requires a fee); not limited to a single employer.

diplomatic.description: An embassy, diplomatic mission, consulate or liaison office of a foreign government or parastatal entity in a host country.

educational_institution.description: An office for an educational institution.

employment_agency.description: An office for an employment service.

energy_supplier.description: An office for an energy supplier.

engineer.description: An office for an engineer or group of engineers.

estate_agent.description: A place where you can rent or buy a house.

event_management.description: An office offering support for event planning and management

financial.description: An office of a company in the financial sector.

financial_advisor.description: A professional who offers financial planning and sells financial services to clients.

geodesist.description: An office of scientists studying Earth shape.

gongo.description: An office of a government-organized non-governmental organization.

government.description: An office of a (supra)national, regional or local government agency or department

graphic_design.description: An office of a graphic designer.

guide.description: An office of tour guides, mountain guides, hiking guides, dive guides, etc.

insurance.description: An office at which you can take out insurance policies.

logistics.description: An office for a company offering logistics.

moving_company.description: An office which offers a relocation service.

ngo.description: An office for a non-profit, non-governmental organisation (NGO).

notary.description: An office for a notary public (common law)

property_management.description: Office of a property rental company for residential apartments or commercial office space or any other property.

quango.description: An office of a quasi-autonomous non-governmental organisation.

religion.description: An office of a community of faith.

research.description: An office for research and development

school.description: An office for a certain subdomain of a school.

security.description: An office for private security guards.

surveyor.description: An office of a person doing technical surveys, such as land surveys or risk and damage evaluations of properties and equipment.

tax_advisor.description: An office for a financial expert specially trained in tax law

telecommunication.description: An office for a telecommunication company.

transport.description: An office of a freight forwarder or transport company.

tutoring.description: An office of tutor or tutoring centre.

union.description: An office of a trade union, an association of workers forming a bargaining unit.

university.description: An office providing services to university students or staff, or the office of a department / faculty in a university.

visa.description: An office of an organisation or business which offers visa assistance.

water_utility.description: The office for a water utility company or water board.

yes.description: Generic tag for unspecified office type.

[en].description: Optometrist; If linked with a shop please useshop=opticianinstead.
A paddling pool is a shallow pool, typically less than 30cm (1 ft.) deep, for children to play in.
Someone who practicesphysical therapy(orkinesiotherapyin French-speaking countries) but is not amedical practitioner.

allotments.description: A separate settlement, which is located outside an officially inhabited locality and has its own addressing

archipelago.description: A named group or chain of closely related islands and islets.

borough.description: A part in a larger city grouped into administrative unit.

city.description: The largest urban settlement or settlements within the territory.

city_block.description: A named city block, usually surrounded by streets.

continent.description: One of the seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, South America.

country.description: A nation state or other high-level national political/administrative area.

county.description: A county - a geographical region of a country.

district.description: A district – a type of administrative division that, in some countries, is managed by local government.

hamlet.description: A smaller rural community, typically with fewer than 100-200 inhabitants, and little infrastructure.

island.description: Any piece of land that is completely surrounded by water and isolated from other significant landmasses (bigger than 1 km²).

islet.description: A very small island (smaller than 1 km²).

isolated_dwelling.description: The smallest kind of settlement (1-2 households).

locality.description: A named place that has no population.

municipality.description: A municipality - single urban administrative division having corporate status.

neighbourhood.description: A neighbourhood is a smaller named, geographically localised place within a suburb of a larger city or within a town or village

ocean.description: The world's five main major oceanic divisions.

plot.description: A named plot is a tract or parcel of land owned or meant to be owned by some owner.

polder.description: A land area that forms an artificial hydrological entity enclosed by embankments and usually is under sea level.

province.description: A subdivision of a country similar to a state.

quarter.description: A quarter is a named, geographically localised place within a suburb of a larger city or within a town, which is bigger than a neighbourhood.

region.description: Used both as a broad tag for geographic or historical areas with no clear boundary and for distinct administration areas (with specific boundaries) in some countries.

sea.description: A large body of salt water part of, or connected to, an ocean.

square.description: A town or village square: a (typically) paved open space, generally of architectural significance, which is surrounded by buildings in a built-up area such as a city, town or village.

subdistrict.description: A subdistrict - a subdivision of a district used for administrative or other purposes.

suburb.description: A part of a town or city with a well-known name and often a distinct identity.

town.description: An important urban centre, between a village and a city in size.

village.description: A smaller distinct settlement, smaller than a town with few facilities available with people traveling to nearby towns to access these.

[en].description: Someone who practicespodiatrybut is not aPhysicianon Wikipedia.

cable.description: An insulated cable carrying electrical power, such as transmission or distribution cables located underground and undersea cables

catenary_mast.description: A pole supporting the overhead wires used to supply electricity to vehicles equipped with a pantograph such as trams and trains.

compensator.description: A static power device used to ensure power quality and electrical network resilience.

connection.description: A free-standing electrical connection between two or more power lines or cables, not on power tower/pole/portal etc.

converter.description: A device to convert power between alternating and direct current electrical power: often, but not only, over high voltage networks

generator.description: A device which converts one form of energy to another, for example, an electrical generator.

insulator.description: An electrical insulator which connects a power line to a support structure and prevents grounding.

inverter.description: A device to convert power from direct current to alternating current.

line.description: High-voltage power lines used for power transmission, usually supported by towers or pylons

minor_line.description: Minor power lines forming the distribution grid, usually carried by poles.

plant.description: An industrial facility where power (electricity, useful heat, biogas) is produced by individual power generator units.

pole.description: A single pole supporting power lines, often a wood, steel, or concrete mast designed to carry minor power lines.

portal.description: A supporting structure for power lines, composed of vertical legs with cables between them attached to a horizontal crossarm.

substation.description: A facility which controls the flow of electricity in a power network with transformers, switchgear or compensators.

switch.description: A device which allows electrical network operators to power up & down lines and transformers in substations or along the power grid.

switchgear.description: A switchgear, which comprises one or more busbar assemblies and a number of bays each connecting a circuit to the busbar assembly.

terminal.description: A point of connection where an overhead power line ends on a building or wall; for example, when connecting it to an indoor substation.

tower.description: A tower or pylon carrying high voltage electricity cables. Often constructed from steel latticework but tubular or solid pylons are also used.

transformer.description: A device for stepping up or down electric voltage. Large power transformers are typically located inside substations

[en].description: Someone who practicespsychotherapybut is not aPhysicianon Wikipedia.

platform.description: This is parallel to the rail line for showing where the actual platforms are. It is also to know where you can change platform and enter the station, so use footpaths to connect them. This is really useful for routing too. Use only if the platform is served by public transport.

stop_area.description: A relation that contains all elements of a train, subway, monorail, tram, bus, trolleybus, aerialway, or ferry stop.

stop_area_group.description: A relation that contains stop areas that make up a public transport interchange.

stop_position.description: The position on the railway track where the train (its centre) stops at a platform. This is useful for routing on long platforms where also short trains stop and on long platforms where multiple trains stop behind each other. See alsopublic_transport=stop_area.

abandoned.description: The course of a former railway which has been abandoned and

buffer_stop.description: Stops the train at the end of a track. Seebuffer stop.

disused.description: A section of railway which is no longer used but where the track and infrastructure remains in place. The track is likely overgrown with endemic vegetation, but could be brought back into useful service with only minor effort.

crossing.description: A point where pedestrians may cross.

derail.description: A device used to prevent fouling of a rail track by unauthorized movements of trains or unattended rolling stock. SeeDerail (railroad).

funicular.description: Cable driven inclined railways on a steep slope, with a pair of cars connected by one cable

level_crossing.description: A point where rails and roads cross.

light_rail.description: A higher-standard tram system, normally in its own right-of-way. Often it connects towns and thus reaches a considerable length (tens of kilometres).

miniature.description: Miniature railwaysare narrower than narrow gauge and carry passengers, frequently at an exact scale of "standard-sized" rail (for example "1/4 scale").  They can often be found in parks.

monorail.description: A railway with only a single rail. A monorail can run above the rail like in Las Vegas andDisneylandor can suspend below the rail like theWuppertal Schwebebahn(Germany).

narrow_gauge.description: Narrow-gauge passenger or freight train tracks. Narrow gauge railways can have mainline railway service like theRhaetian Railwayin Switzerland or can be a small light industrial railway. Usegauge=*to specify the actual width of rails in mm.

platform.description: This is parallel to the rail line for showing where the actual platforms are. It is also to know where you can change platform and enter the station, so use footpaths to connect them. This is really useful for routing too. Use in addition topublic_transport=platform.

preserved.description: This is the old tag for historic trains. Userailway:preserved=yesfor historical railway instead.

proposed.description: Railway being proposed but haven't started construction. Please see theproposedpage regarding verifiability on the ground of this value.

rail.description: Full sized passenger or freight train tracks in the standard gauge for the country or state.

roundhouse.description: A semicircular building with many stalls for servicing engines.

station.description: Railwaypassenger and/or cargostation. Use in addition topublic_transport=station.

stop.description: Marks the position where trains stop at a station. It should be added as a node on a railway=rail way.

subway.description: A city passenger rail service running mostly grade separated (seeWikipedia:rapid transit).  Often a significant portion of the line or its system/network is underground.

subway_entrance.description: The entrance to a subway station, usually going from surface to underground.

tram.description: One or two carriage rail vehicle tracks, usually sharing motor road, sometimes called "street running" (Other languages).

tram_level_crossing.description: A point where trams and roads cross.

tram_stop.description: A tram stop is a place where a passenger can embark / disembark a tram.

traverser.description: These are used for changing trains between railways. Also known as transfer table.

turntable.description: These are used for changing the direction that part of a train is pointing in.

ventilation_shaft.description: A structure that allows ventilation in underground tunnels

water_crane.description: A structure to deliver water to steam locomotives

workshop.description: Repair shop' for (rail) rolling stock' is a place where maintenance or overhaul of these vehicles (rail vehicles, metro, tram, light rail, monorail) is carried out.  It should be added as a area on a building=yes.

[en].description: Medical rehabilitation facility; should not be used for a wellness clinic or hotel.

bicycle.description: Cycle routes or bicycles route are named, numbered or otherwise signed. May go along roads, trails or dedicated cycle paths

[en].description: Site or dedicated healthcare facility where samples of blood/urine/etc are obtained or collected for purpose of analysing them for healthcare diagnostics.

agrarian.description: Shop focused on selling agrarian products, like seeds, agricultural machinery, animal feed, etc.

alcohol.description: Shop selling alcohol to take away

anime.description: Shop focused on selling anime stuff.

art.description: Shop which sells works of art. May be paintings, sculpture, or other types of art

atv.description: Shop focused on sellingatvs(quads), atv equipment and may rent or repair them

baby_goods.description: Shop focused on selling objects for babies (clothes, prams, cots, toys).

beauty.description: A non-hairdresser beauty shop, spa, nail salon, etc.. See alsoshop=hairdresser.

bookmaker.description: A shop that takes bets on sporting and other events at agreed upon odds.

charity.description: A charity shop is a shop operated by a charity, for the purposes of fundraising.

computer.description: Shop focused on selling computers, peripherals, software, …

department_store.description: A single large store – often multiple storeys high – selling a large variety of goods (see alsoshop=mall)

[en].description: Someone who practicesspeech and language therapy.

cable_landing_station.description: A facility where submarine telecommunications cables terminate on land.

connection_point.description: Last point of telecom local loops allowing direct connections towards a few households and subscribers

data_center.description: A building used to house computers and network equipment.

distribution_point.description: Equipment, often small boxes, allowing to directly connect up to 15 individuals or households to a single upstream local loop cable towards telecom exchanges

exchange.description: A place to connect landlines subscribers to telecommunication service equipments

service_device.description: Active equipment which deliver service to connected customers. It can be for instance a DSLAM, a telephone switch, or more recently an OLT for optical networks. It excludes amplifiers or repeaters installed along local loops or long distance lines.

alpine_hut.description: A remote building located in the mountains intended to provide board and lodging.

apartment.description: A furnished apartment or flat with cooking and bathroom facilities that can be rented for holiday vacations.

aquarium.description: A facility with living aquatic animals for public viewing.

attraction.description: An object of interest for a tourist, or a purpose-built tourist attraction

camp_pitch.description: A tent or caravan pitch location within a campsite or caravan site.

camp_site.description: An area where people can camp overnight using tents, camper vans, or caravans.

caravan_site.description: A place where you can stay in a caravan overnight or for longer periods.

chalet.description: A holiday cottage with self-contained cooking and bathroom facilities.

gallery.description: An area or building that displays a variety of visual art exhibitions.

guest_house.description: Accommodation smaller than a hotel and typically owner-operated, such as bed&breakfasts etc.

hotel.description: Hotel – an establishment that provides paid lodging.

information.description: Information for tourists and visitors, including information offices

motel.description: Short term accommodation, particularly for people travelling by car.

museum.description: A museum: an institution with exhibitions on scientific, historical, cultural topics

picnic_site.description: A locality that is suitable for outdoors eating, with facilities to aid a picnic such as tables and benches.

theme_park.description: An amusement park where entertainment is provided by rides, games, concessions.

viewpoint.description: A place worth visiting, often high, with a good view of surrounding countryside or notable buildings.

wilderness_hut.description: A remote building, typically unserviced, providing temporary shelter and sleeping accommodation.

zoo.description: A zoological garden, where animals are confined for viewing by the public.

All commonly used values.description: All commonly used valuesaccording toTaginfo, generallybuilding types

[en].description: a healthcare facility specifically dedicated to administering vaccinations to individuals, to provide immunisation against infectious diseases.

basin.description: An area of land artificially graded to hold water. Includes also structures typically without water.

canal.description: The area covered by the water of a canal

ditch.description: The water covered area of a waterway=ditch - a simple artificial waterway serving as a barrier or for draining surrounding land.

fish_pass.description: Area of a fish pass for the fish to pass waterway dams or weirs. Should contain tag:waterway=fish_pass way inside.

lagoon.description: A body of shallow sea water or brackish water separated from the sea by a barrier.

lake.description: A natural or semi-natural body of relatively still fresh or salt water which is surrounded by land.

lock.description: Water area of the lock chamber.

moat.description: A deep, wide defensive ditch, normally filled with water, dug to surround a fortified habitation.

oxbow.description: U-shaped body of water formed when a wide meander from the main stem of a river is cut off to create a lake.

pond.description: A small body of standing water, man-made in most cases

reflecting_pool.description: A shallow pool of water, undisturbed by fountain jets, for a calm reflective surface, often found in gardens, squares or at memorial sites

reservoir.description: An artificial lake which may be used to store water. Usually formed by a dam over a natural water course.

river.description: The water covered area of a river.

stream_pool.description: A stream pool: a small but relatively deep collection of fresh water, occurring along a narrow stream.

wastewater.description: A clarifier or settling basin of a wastewater treatment plant.

boatyard.description: A place for constructing, repairing and storing vessels out of the water.

canal.description: An artificial open flow waterway used to carry useful water for transportation, waterpower, or irrigation.

dam.description: A barrier built across a river or stream to block and regulate the flow of the river.

ditch.description: A small artificial drainage waterway, usually unlined.

dock.description: An enclosed area for ships or boats within which the height of the water can be managed, or a dry dock.

drain.description: An artificial free flow waterway used for carrying superfluous water, usually lined with concrete.

fairway.description: A navigable route in a lake or sea, often marked by buoys or beacons.

lock_gate.description: A gate of a lock used to raise or lower boats.

pressurised.description: An artificial waterway where water flows in a closed space without air

river.description: The linear flow of a river, in flow direction.

riverbank.description: Using this tag is discouraged, use tag:natural=water + tag:water=river instead.

soakhole.description: The point at which a river or stream percolates into or through the soil

stream.description: A naturally-forming waterway that is too narrow to be classed as a river.

tidal_channel.description: A natural intertidal waterway in mangroves, salt marshes, and tidal flats with water flow in the direction of the tide.

turning_point.description: A place to turn the driving direction for vessels, where the boats are longer than the river/canal is wide.

water_point.description: A place to fill fresh water holding tanks of a boat.

waterfall.description: A place where water flows over a vertical drop or a series of drops in the course of a stream or river.

weir.description: A barrier built across a river to control water speed and depth. Designed to let water flow over the top all the time or at least in specific conditions.
